source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt: Design the primary airframe of an autonomous aerial robotics benchmark as a multi-part bonded/bolted assembly consisting of the central CFRP laminate hub plate, eight CFRP pultruded arm tubes cantilevered from the hub, eight motor mount plates at the arm tips, eight PA12-printed hub-to-arm joint clamps, a payload attachment bracket at centerline, and the bolt/fastener set. Inheriting a prior-mission payload and duration envelope, the vehicle must autonomously transport a 2 kg payload module to a ship mast 3 km offshore, deposit it with precision, and return home within a 9-minute flight window, all without GPS assistance once over water. Target configuration: coaxial octocopter, MTOW 12 kg, empty weight 8 kg, 4 kg battery (6S2P Li-ion 22000 mAh), 2 kg payload. Rotor diameter 0.66 m (26 inch) x 8 rotors; hover thrust per rotor 15 N, peak thrust 30 N. Airframe is carbon-fiber tube + printed PA12 joints: main tubes 25 mm OD x 22 mm ID CFRP pultruded (E = 140 GPa, Poisson 0.30, tensile 1500 MPa, compressive 900 MPa, density 1600 kg/m^3) length 500 mm cantilever from central hub, hub plate 2 mm CFRP laminate. Load cases: (LC1) hover at MTOW: 12 kg x 9.81 m/s^2 / 8 rotors = 14.7 N each; (LC2) peak evasive maneuver: 2 g lateral + 1.5 g vertical on full 12 kg; (LC3) 3 km out-and-back endurance: 9 minutes at 15 m/s cruise = 540 s of aerodynamic drag 10 N at body center; (LC4) wind gust: 15 m/s cross-gust encounter produces +/- 30% rotor thrust differential at one rotor = 9 N alternating; (LC5) payload release: sudden 2 kg mass release during precision drop = 19.6 N step-change at payload attachment point; (LC6) saltwater landing contingency (out-of-spec): 2 g on one arm during emergency water impact. Pass/fail: (R1) max ply strain in arms under LC1-LC4 <= 0.4%; (R2) hub plate strain under LC2 <= 0.3%; (R3) arm tip deflection under LC2 <= 15 mm; (R4) first arm bending mode above 40 Hz; (R5) total airframe mass (tubes + hub + motor mounts) <= 2.0 kg; (R6) linear buckling eigenvalue under LC2 compression phase >= 2.5. Verification is performed on the assembled hub + 8 arms + motor mounts + payload bracket multi-body model with representative bonded/bolted joint stiffness; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Deliverables: STEP, FEA per case, modal, buckling, mass report.
short_prompt:
Design the primary airframe of an autonomous aerial robotics benchmark as a multi-part bonded/bolted assembly (central CFRP hub plate + eight CFRP pultruded arm tubes + eight motor-mount plates + eight PA12-printed hub-to-arm clamps + payload bracket + fasteners).

Mission: transport a 2 kg payload 3 km to a ship mast, deposit, and return within 9 minutes without GPS over water. Coaxial octocopter, MTOW 12 kg, empty 8 kg, 4 kg battery (6S2P 22000 mAh), 2 kg payload, rotor dia 0.66 m (26 inch) x 8. Hover thrust 15 N per rotor, peak 30 N. Main tubes 25 mm OD x 22 mm ID pultruded CFRP (E 140 GPa, Poisson 0.30, tensile 1500 MPa, compressive 900 MPa, density 1600 kg/m^3), 500 mm cantilever; hub plate 2 mm CFRP laminate.

Load cases.
- LC1 hover at MTOW: 12 kg x 9.81 / 8 = 14.7 N per rotor.
- LC2 peak evasive: 2 g lateral + 1.5 g vertical on 12 kg.
- LC3 cruise drag: 9 minutes at 15 m/s = 540 s of 10 N drag at body center.
- LC4 gust: 15 m/s cross-gust produces +/- 30% thrust differential = 9 N alternating at one rotor.
- LC5 payload release: 2 kg step change = 19.6 N at payload attachment.
- LC6 saltwater landing (out-of-spec): 2 g on one arm.

Arm tip deflection under LC2 <= 15 mm; first arm bending mode > 40 Hz; buckling eigenvalue under LC2 >= 2.5.

prompt.title: IARC autonomous aerial robot airframe (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Coaxial octocopter airframe: CFRP hub plate + 8 arm tubes + 8 motor mount plates + 8 PA12 joint clamps + payload bracket + fasteners, 12 kg MTOW.

parts.name: central_hub_plate
parts.role: CFRP laminate central hub plate supporting the 8 radial arms and battery/payload

parts.name: arm_tube
parts.role: CFRP pultruded tube cantilevered 500 mm from the hub to each of 8 motor stations

parts.name: motor_mount_plate
parts.role: motor mount plate at each arm tip for the coaxial motor pair

parts.name: hub_to_arm_joint_clamp
parts.role: PA12-printed joint clamp tying each arm tube root into the hub plate

parts.name: payload_attachment_bracket
parts.role: centerline payload attachment bracket carrying the 2 kg payload module and LC5 release step

parts.name: fastener_set
parts.role: M3 fasteners tying joint clamps, motor mounts, and payload bracket into the assembly

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox: ~1.1 m tip-to-tip span (hub + 2 x 500 mm arms)
assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: +Z vertical, 8 arms at 45 deg increments about hub center

boundary_conditions.interfaces: arm tube roots bonded + clamped into PA12 joint clamps bolted to hub plate; motor mount plates bonded + bolted to arm tips; payload bracket bolted to hub plate; fasteners modelled as representative stiffness
boundary_conditions.support: hub center fixed for free-free modal; each motor mount loaded with LC1-LC4 rotor loads

prompt.objective: minimize airframe mass under 2.0 kg while passing ply strain, deflection, modal, and buckling gates

interfaces.load_path: PA12 joint clamps bolted to hub plate at 8 radial positions
arm tube root bonded + clamped into PA12 joint clamp
motor mount plate bonded + bolted to arm tip
payload attachment bracket bolted to hub plate centerline
fastener clamp force at motor mount / joint clamp interfaces

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: Autonomous aerial robots of this class use a CFRP hub plate, radial pultruded tubes, printed joint clamps, and motor mount plates; rotor thrust, evasive maneuver, and payload-release loads cross bonded and bolted joints that must be modelled as a multi-body airframe.
notes.speculation_caveats: Joint-clamp material (PA12), payload bracket placement, and fastener count are speculative; tube material, loads, and thresholds inherit the original sample verbatim.
